description_long: This is a large, multi-engine airplane with a straight, high-mounted wing configuration and a robust, boxy fuselage. The wings are adorned with red star insignias, suggesting a military or historical Soviet design. The aircraft features four engines mounted on the leading edge of the wing, each with visible propellers. The tail section is distinctive, with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted high, forming an H-tail configuration. The landing gear is fixed and consists of multiple wheels, visible beneath the wings and fuselage. The cockpit is positioned at the very front of the fuselage, with a multi-windowed, enclosed design for enhanced visibility.
description: A large, high-wing, multi-engine airplane with red star insignias, a boxy fuselage, and a prominent H-tail. Features four propeller engines and a forward, enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide wingspan, four propeller engines, and the forward cockpit with multiple windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the high-mounted straight wings, red star insignias, and the boxy fuselage with the H-tail visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the H-tail configuration, the full wingspan, and the landing gear beneath the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same high-wing, multi-engine layout, and the distinctive tail and cockpit placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted on the leading edge of the wing, each with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed landing gear with multiple main wheels under the wings and fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
